He then goes on to admit that far from being like his on-screen counterpart, he’s actually a “Maxxinisto” and, thanks to TJ Maxx, a “very well-dressed” one at that.
“While certain fictional characters may have spoken hastily, I personally have developed a taste for elevated quality without the unnecessary drama of elevated prices,” he sums up in the ad.

While Tucci and Nigel are very different in real life, Tucci clearly shares his character’s love of fine fashion, which begs the question: Is there anything in Tucci’s closet that he considers a serious shopping find?
“I have this jacket. It’s a black leather jacket that is a bit longer,” he says of his score. “It’s really thick and it’s really old.”
According to Tucci, he discovered the one-of-a-kind vintage leather coat in a thrift shop.
“As it turns out, it’s a Chicago policeman’s jacket from the 1940s and it’s made out of horse hide. That’s why it’s so incredibly thick,” explains Tucci.

“I have it in my closet. I seldom wear it, but it’s like, to me, one of the coolest looking ... the leather’s all weathered and messed up, but I can’t get rid of it,” he adds. “I almost want to frame it. You can still see the little shadow or imprint of where the badge was.”
And beyond their mutual love of fashion, Tucci does have some very Nigel-like thoughts on fashion faux pas, including a disdain for athleisure.
“Wear athletic wear when you’re working out – no other time,” Tucci told GQ last month at the premiere of “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”
And he stands by the advice while talking with TODAY.com.
“It’s fine if you’ve just exercised and you have to run to the shop to get something. But, otherwise, no,” says Tucci of the trend of wearing workout clothes in place of more traditional daily wear.
So, is it fair to say that when it comes to good style, fashion wins over comfort?
“Without question,” says Tucci. “It’s not even a question.”

title: Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy to Run Amok in ‘Hocus Pocus 3’
summary: Witches Winifred, Sarah and Mary will again bring chaos and mischief to the world in the upcoming “Hocus Pocus 3.”Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy will reprise their roles as the Sanderson Sisters in the movie, which is in early development at Disney Live Action Studios, TODAY.com has learned.
story: Witches Winifred, Sarah and Mary will again bring chaos and mischief to the world in the upcoming “Hocus Pocus 3.”

Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy will reprise their roles as the Sanderson Sisters in the movie, which is in early development at Disney Live Action Studios, TODAY.com has learned. Deadline first reported the news of the third installment and the possibility that the film will have a theatrical distribution following “Hocus Pocus 2.” The 2022 sequel, also starring Midler, Parker and Najimy, was released on Disney+.

The sisters first wreaked havoc on Salem residents in the original 1993 flick, which has since become a beloved Halloween classic. The movie followed a teenager who accidentally awakened and freed a coven of evil witches who lay dormant in an abandoned house. Nearly 30 years later, another group of naive teenagers brought the witches to life once more.
Fans were delighted to see the trio together again in 2022, and last year the stars gave them hope that another installment was on the way.
In July 2025, Parker appeared on “Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen” and revealed that the cast was in talks for “Hocus Pocus 3.”
“No more developments other than we would like to do it,” she said when responding to a fan’s question about the threequel’s status. “We’ve been having some conversations.”

Then a few months later in October 2025, just in time for Halloween, Midler also stopped by Andy Cohen’s talk show and discussed a third film, confirming that a script has already been written thanks to the success of the sequel.
She said “Hocus Pocus 2” amassed “billions of watches” on the streaming platform. “Billions, I’d never heard that number before,” she added.

When asked about the next movie, she said, “They sent a script and a lot of it was brilliant. So I got very excited. And now we’re sort of, like, trying to figure out what it is and where it’s going to be and how much it’s going to cost.”
She said “all those logistical things” were still being decided.

As for Najimy, she gave fans hope that they will see “Hocus Pocus 3” soon when she posted a picture of her Halloween costume on Instagram in October 2025. Najimy dressed as a witch in an ensemble that Mary would surely approve of, donning her twisted wig.
In the comments, the Disney+ official account replied, “Happy Halloween to our Mary Sanderson.”
Midler, Parker and Najimy have yet to comment on the latest news about their next team up.

title: What Jane Fonda and Her ‘Favorite Ex-Husband’ Ted Turner Said About Each Other
summary: Jane Fonda and Ted Turner were married for a decade, sharing words of encouragement, love and support over the years.
story: Jane Fonda and Ted Turner were one of Hollywood’s hottest couples in the 1990s, but the two grew even closer after they divorced.
Fonda, 88, often jokingly referred to the billionaire media mogul and CNN founder, who died May 6 at age 87 following a 2018 diagnosis of Lewy body dementia, as her “favorite ex-husband.”
Shortly after reports of his death, Fonda penned an emotional tribute to Turner of her “immediate thoughts.”

“(Ted) swept into my life, a gloriously handsome, deeply romantic, swashbuckling pirate and I’ve never been the same,” she began in a lengthy Instagram post that included a picture of the former couple embracing. “He needed me. No one had ever let me know they needed me, and this wasn’t your average human being that needed me, this was the creator of CNN, and Turner Classic Movies, who had won the America’s Cup as the world’s greatest sailor. He had a big life, a brilliant mind and a soaring sense of humor.”
Fonda continued to gush about the late mogul, adding that he took care of her and made her believe in herself.

“He gave me confidence. I think I did the same for him, but that’s what women are raised to do. Men like Ted aren’t supposed to express need and vulnerability. That was Ted’s greatest strength, I believe,” she said.
She added that he also taught her about nature, wildlife, business and other subjects thanks to his wide range of knowledge.
Fonda then complimented Turner for being ambitious, noting he was the most competitive person she knew besides Katharine Hepburn.
“Ted was challenging, but I’ve always been up for a challenge, and with Ted it was almost always worth it,” Fonda wrote.
Her tribute continued in the comments. She said she deeply loved Turner and can picture him in heaven surrounded by wildlife. She also mentioned the love she has for his five children.
She concluded, “Rest in Peace, dearest Ted. You are loved and you will be remembered.”
The unlikely couple, who came from opposite sides of the political aisle, were married from 1991 to 2001.

Fonda, an outspoken liberal activist who grew up the daughter of Hollywood legend Henry Fonda, routinely praised Turner, an Atlanta-based businessman who liked to hunt, in interviews for decades after their split.
“He’s outrageous and hysterically funny,” she told People about her ex in 2023.
Fonda, who, like Turner, had been married twice before, also wrote lovingly of the good times the couple shared in her 2005 memoir, “My Life So Far.”

“There were times when something would set us to laughing so hard we’d sink to the floor, like the night when our guffaws collapsed us at the foot of the ‘Gone With the Wind’ staircase at his plantation and we had to crawl up to bed on hands and knees,” she wrote.
Read on to learn more about what Fonda and Turner have said about each other over the years.
Fonda Was Attracted to Turner’s Energy
Shortly after she and Turner began dating in 1990, Fonda told People she was attracted to the maverick businessman's energy.
“Ted can keep up with me,” she said. “He’s a very, very funny, lovable, complicated person.”
The couple wed at Turner’s 8,100-acre plantation, Avalon, in Capps, Florida, according to People.
They split a decade later, in part, because of Turner’s infidelity, Fonda explained in her memoir.
Turner Recalled Crying After Fonda Left Him
In his own 2008 memoir, “Call Me Ted,” Turner recalled the couple's whirlwind courtship, which began in 1990 when Turner asked Fonda for a date after learning of her divorce from activist Tom Hayden.
Fonda declined, but Turner kept insisting. Six months later, sparks flew when the pair finally met up. They married the following year.
Turner also wrote about the devastation he experienced after Fonda left him.
Returning to his Montana ranch for the first time after their split, Turner saw Fonda had removed her belongings.
“Our closets faced each other’s, and when I saw her empty space I sat down on the floor between them and cried,” he wrote.
Turner Called Fonda the Love of His Life
In 2012, CNN’s Piers Morgan asked Turner if Fonda was the “great love of his life.”
“Probably,” Turner responded.
After more prompting from Morgan, Turner revealed he’d never gotten over losing Fonda and he knew he never would.
“When you love somebody and you really love them, you never stop loving them no matter how hard you try, you can’t stop,” Turner explained.
“There’s nothing wrong with that. That’s good,” he added.
In Fonda’s 2018 documentary “Jane Fonda in Five Acts,” Turner described their separation as “very difficult,” adding, “I thought it was a mistake.”
“I’ve survived and so has she, but I feel like I was happier when I was with her than subsequently,” he said.
Fonda Called Turner ‘Sexy’ and ‘Brilliant’ Years After Their Split
In that same documentary, Fonda recalled the painful end of her and Turner’s marriage.
“Ted was a genius... but he was also gorgeous. I mean, he was really a handsome man, and very, very sexy,” Fonda said, sharing that she was attracted to his “wild brilliance.”
But she also decided to give up her career while she was with him and eventually felt like she couldn't be her full authentic self.

“We had a wonderful 10 years, but I had to hide a part of me in order to please him,” she said, noting there was a voice in her head telling her, “‘If you stay, you will never be authentic, you will never be able to be whole.’”
Fonda Spoke Lovingly of Turner Months Before His Death
Months before Turner died, Fonda sang his praises when she spoke at the Georgia Campaign for Adolescent Power and Potential (GCAPP) EmPower Party and Gala in Atlanta on Nov. 13, 2025.
Fonda acknowledged how Turner’s contributions helped her establish the organization in 1995, noting it, “never would have happened if it wasn’t for Ted,” People reported at the time.

“It was a very challenging time in Georgia. Had I not been with Ted, this was something, had he not stood by me with his love and support, we never would have survived,” Fonda said of the businessman, whose public appearances declined after his 2018 diagnosis.
“Ted’s not here,” she added, “but he is here in my heart and I know he is here in a lot of our hearts.”

title: Hayden Panettiere Publicly Comes Out as Bisexual
summary: Hayden Panettiere opens up about her sexuality, revealing publicly for the first time that she is bisexual and why she waited to share the news at 36.
story: Hayden Panettiere is publicly discussing her sexuality for the first time, revealing she is bisexual and started dating women “at a very, very young age.”

The 36-year-old actor opens up about her personal life in her upcoming memoir, “This Is Me: A Reckoning,” out May 19. In the book, the “Heroes” alum shares details about her past relationships, including with actor Milo Ventimiglia and her former fiancé Wladimir Klitschko, while also addressing her sexuality.
During an interview with Us Weekly published May 6, Panettiere was asked about coming out in the book and her attraction to women.

“That’s something about me I was never able to share with the world, because it was just never the right time,” she said about waiting to publicly label her sexuality. “It was either I was too young, and I was being forced to be perfect at all times. I was not encouraged to just be myself.”

She continued, “Then came the period where it felt like people coming out, especially women, saying that they were bisexual or liked girls, was a fad. I was afraid that if I was honest, it was going to be like me jumping on the bandwagon.”
The actor recalled struggling to find the words to express how she felt.
“It’s sad I had to wait until I was 36 years old to share that part of me, but better late than never, right?” she said.
With her book release coming up soon, Panettiere feels free to embrace all sides of herself.
She added, “I’m comfortable to confidently say that yes, I am bisexual. I said it! This is the first time I got to say it out loud.”
Panettiere told the magazine she has never been in love with a woman. As a celebrity, she found it “scary” to date women in the past.

“It was scary, though, because there were paparazzi always waiting for me outside, to follow me everywhere. I had very little privacy,” she explained. “I have dated women. I was much more into women even as a child than I was men. I have explored it, but because I hadn’t shared this with anybody, I didn’t really have the courage to throw myself fully emotionally into it.”
She said she feared falling in love because she didn’t want to have to hide that relationship from the world.

Speaking more about her decision to come out, Panettiere said it took her two years to write her memoir. During that time, stories about her sexuality came up and she realized she wanted to be “brutally honest.” But, she was still worried.
“And once again, it was always the fear of not being perfect, and what my team was going to think about it, what the public’s opinion was going to be about it,” she told Us Weekly.
She also said she began dating women “at a very, very young age.”
However, all her public relationships were with men. She was engaged to Klitschko, a Ukrainian former professional boxer, from 2013 to 2018. The two share 11-year-old daughter Kaya.
Panettiere has spoken candidly in the past about her struggles and how her life mirrored the alcoholism and postpartum depression storylines her character experienced on “Nashville.”
But in her latest interview, she confirmed that is in a better place and has an “incredible” relationship with her ex and daughter.

“I’m so grateful for Wlad. We’re very close, and have had a deep friendship, along with a relationship, since I met him when I was 19. So to see that we still have this bond and the ability to co-parent and show our daughter (that) even though we’re not together, doesn’t mean that there’s a lack of love here, and it doesn’t mean that you’re unsafe,” she shared.

Praising Kaya, she gushed, “And my daughter, she’s just an incredible gift. She’s otherworldly and so kind and so fierce and surrounded by good people, and it’s incredible for me to be able to watch her thrive in areas that I struggled with growing up. She’s just the best thing, all the best parts of me and Wlad.”

title: The Best Dressed Stars of the 2026 Met Gala
summary: Who were the best dressed celebrities from the 2026 Met Gala? Two fashion experts shared their picks, including Connor Storrie, Rihanna and Eileen Gu.
story: The 2026 Met Gala may be over, but some of the best dressed A-list celebrities now linger in fashion lovers’ minds.

The gala is held every year to raise funds for the Metropolitan Museum of Art’s Costume Institute. This year’s gala theme was inspired by the institute’s spring exhibition: “Costume Art.” The gala’s dress code? The enticing “Fashion is Art.”
Revisit our recap of the 2026 Met Gala

“Why’d They Wear That?” host and QVC ambassador Stacy London joins Julie Matos, a New York City-based celebrity stylist, and Maggie Gillette, a fashion expert and partner at the Curio, to break down some of their favorite looks from the 2026 Met Gala.
Chase Infiniti
Chase Infiniti’s colorful Thom Browne gown, which was inspired by Alexandros of Antioch’s ancient Greek marble sculpture Venus de Milo, was one of Stacy London’s favorite looks for the night.
“Chase proves that color is a serious trend. Not just one color … all the colors! She is truly dressing for joy,” the TV personality tells TODAY.com.
Grace Gummer
London also gave top marks to “Love Story” star Grace Gummer’s golden off-the-shoulder gown by designer Gabriela Hearst.
“I like Grace’s look because it’s the less obvious body sculpture look. It blends more fashion in with art form rather than fashion reproducing art,” says London.
Gabrielle Union
Another hit in London’s book? Gabrielle Union’s sheer, body-hugging Michael Kors gown embellished with a whopping 170,000 crystals.
Union topped off the look with a majestic crown hairstyle and Tiffany & Co. necklace.
“Mesh sparkle and more sheer looks modern in a simple but dramatic silhouette. Also, HELLO body con!” says London.
Rihanna
Rihanna arrived fashionably late (again!) to this year’s gala on the arm of her longtime love, rapper A$AP Rocky.
The “Umbrella” singer’s wild gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela, combined with large pieces from Briony Raymond and Dyne jewelry, gets top marks from Matos.

“Rihanna closed the night with absolute authority,” Matos tells TODAY.com. “The sculptural, high-shine gown, finished with dramatic volume and a sweeping train, felt both controlled and expansive, less an outfit, more of a statement.”
Adut Akech
Pregnant model Adut Akech, who turned to designer Thom Browne for an elegant expectant look, is at the top of Gillette’s list.
“Perhaps the chicest maternity look I’ve ever seen,” Gillette tells TODAY.com. “I love the way that Thom Browne created a dress that was at once structured and organic feeling.
“The ring of delicate flowers circling Akech’s stomach perfectly highlighted the pregnant body while celebrating the creativity inherent in bringing new life to the world.”
Connor Storrie
“Heated Rivalry” star Connor Storrie’s custom Yves Saint Laurent look also made London and Gillette’s lists.
“This look was really well balanced. That suit was impeccable,” Gillette explains.

“When I first saw the outfit, Storrie had the jacket on and I thought that he just had a scarf as a piece of ‘flair.’ But later it became apparent it was a beautiful top which he accented with a pin for a pop of color.”
Adds London, “I just love Connor’s arms in this look.”
Naomi Osaka

Tennis champ Naomi Osaka’s dramatic white custom two-piece set from Robert Wun won enthusiastic raves from Matos, who says the look “felt highly detailed and almost cinematic, with layered textures and intricate construction that read as a complete work of art.”
Janelle Monae
Musician Janelle Monae’s wild Christian Siriano gown combined electrical cables and circuit board gadgetry with green moss and butterflies for a commentary on nature and technology.
Matos, for one, loved it.

“Janelle Monae delivered a conceptual Siriano look, blending structured tailoring with both organic and technical details,” she tells TODAY.com. “The wires, texture, and sculptural detail gave it a futuristic, almost AI-driven quality.”
Tory Burch
While several stars showed up to the gala wearing Tory Burch designs, Burch herself opted for a sophisticated vintage Mugler gown.

“This dress showed that a quiet look can have a presence,” Gillette says. “The gown fit like a glove and the styling was absolute perfection. And when she turned-that little capelet with the extra embellishment just put the look over the top for me.”
Hailey Bieber
Model Hailey Bieber’s gold and blue Saint Laurent gown was a win in London and Gillette’s books.
“While we saw a lot of this literal sculpture bodice, I favored the less literal ones, but this is the exception. Beautiful color,” London tells TODAY.com.
Explains Gillette, “Saint Laurent was known for his multiple references to and collaborations with artists, and the dress that Hailey Bieber was modeled after one of them."

“The gold bodice was a replica of the ones that Saint Laurent created with sculptor artist Claude Lalanne when they made ‘body casts’ of the model Veruschka and it captured the intersection of art and fashion beautifully," adds Gillette.
Emma Chamberlain
Matos and London both flipped for influencer Emma Chamberlain’s custom Mugler gown, which was hand-painted by artist and designer Anna Deller-Yee.

Chamberlain, says Matos, “wore a painted, body-contouring Mugler look that treated the body as canvas. The sculptural lines and trompe l’oeil effect kept it sharp and visually striking, while still feeling controlled.”
Adds London, “I love the way this feels like fashion and art combined. The colors are divine.”
Bad Bunny
Matos also applauded rapper Bad Bunny for boldly showing up in a Zara look combined with prosthetics and make-up to make him look like a much older man.

“Bad Bunny wore a tailored Zara look paired with a full prosthetic transformation, aging himself decades forward. The styling turned the look into a statement on time and identity, pushing it beyond fashion into performance,” she says.
Eileen Gu
Olympic skier and model Eileen Gu’s playful Iris Van Herpen gown was a hit with Gillette.
“Whimsy and joy are so underrated these days and I love that Gu and Van Herpen brought that to the Met with this look,” she explains.
“Of course Van Herpen is also an artist and a meticulous technical genius so while we all see the pretty end result that dress reportedly has 15,000 glass bubbles that took 2,500 hours to create.”
Zoë Kravitz
Zoë Kravitz’s lacy black Saint Laurent dress made Gillette and Matos' lists, with Gillette citing the elegance of the look.
“While this structure of the dress was very traditional, the use of a sheer unlined lace gave it a modernity and lightness that made it feel fresh and youthful,” Gillette tells TODAY.com.
Matos loves how the “exaggerated” hips of Kravitz’s dress “reworked a classic silhouette.”
“It felt cool, precise and entirely self-possessed,” says Matos.
Colman Domingo
Colman Domingo in Valentino stood out as one of the night’s best dressed men, says Matos.
The actor’s “richly colored, patterned Valentino look” felt “bold and graphic,” she says.
“The sharp tailoring and strong palette made it confident without feeling excessive,” adds Matos.
A$AP Rocky
Matos also admired how A$AP Rocky wore a “reworked Chanel look” with “classic tweed elements and layered styling.”
The rapper’s ensemble “felt effortless but considered, merging heritage codes with a modern edge,” she says.
Gracie Abrams
Gillette cited Gracie Abrams as one more star who nailed the Met Gala’s theme this year.
The “That’s So True” singer “absolutely glowed” in her Chanel gown, which was inspired by by a Gustav Klimt painting, says Gillette.
“With clear reference to the famous painter, it hit the fashion as art theme out of the park in the most beautiful way,” she adds.

title: See the Trailer for Season 2 of OWN’s ‘Heart & Hustle: Houston’
summary: Watch the trailer for Season 2 of of OWN’s "Heart & Hustle: Houston" and find out when the new season premieres. Plus, catch up with the cast.
story: “It’s all about sisterhood,” “Heart & Hustle: Houston” star Chloe Cook says in a trailer for the new season. But based on the footage, some friendships might be broken beyond repair.

The OWN reality television series, which follows a group of Black women looking for love and striving for success in Houston, will soon return for Season 2. This season, all the main cast members are back — Cook, La’Torria Lemon, LeBrina Jackson, Muneera Page, Roe Grady-Pichardo and Alaina Saulsberry — to face one other after the fallouts from Season 1.
TODAY.com is exclusively revealing the trailer for Season 2, which shows some of the ladies breaking off into separate groups as they struggle to rebuild their friendships.

In the teaser, the ladies are initially seen celebrating each other and exploring their romantic connections. Next, the clip takes a turn. “Life’s good, but behind the scenes…” Cook begins before the tone changes.

Saulsberry opens up about struggling to keep her family together. Elsewhere, Grady-Pichardo sheds tears as she worries about her children feeling like she is keeping them from their father. An emotional Lemon says, “I feel like my mom gave up. And not only did she give up on everything, she gave up on me.”
Page and Cook confront the men in their lives, with Cook wondering if she was cheated on.
However, the trailer hints that not everything is as it seems. “There are two sides to every story,” Jackson notes.
Some of the women are also not on speaking terms and one is called out for being a “mean girl.”

All the drama will unfold when Season 2 of “Heart & Hustle: Houston” premieres on OWN Saturday, May 30, at 9 p.m. ET/PT. New episodes will then air regularly at 8 p.m. ET/PT starting Saturday, June 20.
Watch the full clip and then get a refresher on everything you need to know about the cast, below.
‘Heart & Hustle: Houston’ Season 2 Cast
The six main cast members of “Heart & Hustle: Houston” are all reuniting for Season 2. Here’s what you need to remember about each member of the friend group and what to expect this season.

Cook is hoping to take the next step in her relationship with Jordan, but trust issues pop up before he can propose. Meanwhile, Cook’s family is skeptical about the couple’s 17-year age gap. As for her career, Cook leans into her passion for event planning and tries to rebuild her business.
Lemon is busy running her award-winning public relations firm and is also looking to expand her philanthropic impact. Privately, she is grieving the loss of her father and is looking to heal.

Jackson wants to further her progress in the wellness space as the owner of an elite spa. It seems she could be the “mean girl” mentioned in the trailer as her relationship with the other women becomes fractured.
Page is managing being a mom, wife and career woman. She and her husband, Norm, are struggling with their emotional connection and she hopes to repair their relationship.
Grady-Pichardo is an innovator and restaurateur that is involved in the Houston nightlife scene. Outside of work, she is a single mother who is co-parenting with her ex, Jayvon.
Saulsberry, the CEO of a makeup company, is comfortable in her work life after building a beauty empire. But outside of work, she and her husband, Josh, are separated and heading toward a divorce.

title: Whitney Leavitt Speaks Out About ‘SLOMW’ Exit and When She’s Leaving
summary: Whitney Leavitt is confirming she will exit “The Secret Lives of Mormon Lives” and when she plans to leave the Hulu reality TV series
story: Whitney Leavitt is confirming she will exit “The Secret Lives of Mormon Lives” and when she plans to leave the Hulu reality TV series.
The 32-year-old actor said in a May 5 selfie video on Instagram that the upcoming fifth season will be her last.

Leavitt’s update comes after she disclosed her exit from the show during her final performance in “Chicago” on Broadway on May 3, according to Deadline. Leavitt had been playing Roxie Hart since her debut in February.

“It’s honestly so crazy to me looking back on this journey because I had been trying to get into theater and film way before ‘Secret Lives’ even came into my life,” Leavitt said in her Instagram video.
She explained that “Secret Lives” fell into her life “organically.”
“It’s definitely not the path that I had envisioned in my mind to get to where I am today, but I wouldn’t change a thing,” she added.

The former MomTok influencer, who has been a cast member on the Hulu series since it premiered in September 2024, went on to say she “experienced so much” with the other women on the show and “learned so much” about herself.

“We have been through so much together, even more than what you’ve seen,” said Leavitt. “We’ve had really big highs, really big lows and no matter what happens with our relationships like that is something that will always be a part of our life, will always be a part of my life, and I wouldn’t change a thing.”
Leavitt concluded by saying she was leaving the reality series “with gratitude.”
“I feel content,” she said. “I feel like this is a chapter that’s closing in my life and honestly, I believe that’s how it was always meant to be.

“I’m so grateful for the ‘Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,’” she added. “It’s got me where I am today. It’s given me the opportunities that you all have seen but I’m ready. I’m ready for the next chapter and I can not wait to share with you guys what’s next.”

Leavitt’s starring role in “Chicago” proved to be wildly successful for the musical. She extended her role in March because of what the show’s producers called “an overwhelming demand” for tickets, People reported at the time. With Leavitt in as the show’s lead, “Chicago” broke records with the highest weekly ticket sales in the show’s 29-year history, according to People.

title: What’s It Like Inside the Met Gala? These Photos Give a Glimpse Behind the Scenes
summary: The Met Gala has long been considered fashion’s biggest night, and the 2026 event certainly lived up to those expectations
story: The Met Gala has long been considered fashion’s biggest night, and the 2026 event certainly lived up to those expectations.
On May 4, Hollywood A-listers stepped out in style to attend the annual charity fundraiser and fashion exhibition at New York’s Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Revisit the all top moments from the 2026 Met Gala

This year’s theme, “Costume Art,” featured a dress code described as “fashion is art.” Watching attendees walk the red carpet is always the highlight of the night for fashion lovers, especially since there’s a no-phones policy inside the event, per The Hollywood Reporter.
But just because celebrities can’t share a look inside, that doesn’t mean there aren’t photographers on hand.

Lucky for us, the professionals captured behind-the-scenes snapshots that revealed a few outfit changes and some fun celebrity pairings. Below, we’re sharing a few of the standout photos of the night.
Sabrina Carpenter's Outfit Changes
On the red carpet, Sabrina Carpenter sported a Dior gown that was made of cascading strips of film and accessorized with a jeweled headpiece.
Inside the event, the singer changed into a vibrant dress with a crisscross neckline and prints of Andy Warhol’s pop art of Marilyn Monroe.

While performing with Stevie Nicks, Carpenter slipped into yet another ensemble: a gold spaghetti strap mini dress with shimmery tassels. Meanwhile, Nicks also looked gorgeous in an embellished black gown.
Kendall Jenner's Wings
Kendall Jenner channeled her inner Greek goddess at the 2026 Met Gala in a Zac Posen gown that was inspired by “Winged Victory of Samothrace,” a statue in the Louvre museum in Paris.
While speaking with E! News’ La La Anthony, Posen said Jenner’s ensemble may include a “surprise” that would make its debut inside the event.
Photographers captured a photo of the stunning surprise: a set of wings that helped her channel the statue even more.
The Fashionable Co-Chairs
Alongside Anna Wintour, the 2026 Met Gala co-chairs included Nicole Kidman, Beyoncé and Venus Williams.
On the red carpet, Kidman opted for a red sequined Chanel gown as she posed for photos alongside daughter Sunday Rose, 17.
Meanwhile, Beyoncé walked the red carpet in an embellished Olivier Rousteing ensemble as she was accompanied by her 14-year-old daughter, Blue Ivy Carter, who made her Met Gala debut.
Williams also understood the assignment and showed up in a Swarovski crystal mesh gown, which she paired with a Wimbledon plate-inspired necklace.

Inside the event, two of the co-chairs changed their outfits. Kidman slipped into a red and cream gown with feathers and embellishments. Beyoncé brought the drama in a black long sleeve gown with sequins for days, a wide train and a veil she wore over her face.
Sza, Doechii and Hailey Bieber

Inside the event, attendees have the opportunity to catch up with old friends and mingle with some new faces. Photographers caught Sza, Doechii and Hailey Bieber together inside and snapped this stunning shot.

Per Vogue, Bieber was sporting a custom Saint Laurent dress with a 24-karat gold sculpted bodice. Doechii rocked a burgundy Marc Jacobs wrap dress and Sza opted for a yellow Bode dress with a larger-than-life headpiece.
Rihanna, Ciara and Katy Perry
One photographer captured this photo of three female powerhouse singers chatting inside the event.
Rihanna, who spoke with TODAY's Sheinelle Jones on the red carpet, wore a sculptural gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela FW25 Couture.

On the red carpet, Perry kept her identity under wraps at first while wearing a mesh and silver mask with her white off-the-shoulder Stella McCartney gown. Inside the event, she took the mask off to pose for a photo.
Ciara also dressed to impress in an Egyptian-inspired gold gown and headpiece.
The ‘Heated Rivalry’ Guys
"Heated Rivalry" stars Connor Storrie and Hudson William appeared to have a blast inside the event. In one photo, Storrie was seen dancing alongside Tate McRae and Blackpink singer Rosé.
Williams also mingled with a member of Blackpink, Lisa, at the event and showed off his keen fashion sense in a custom Balenciaga look.

title: Tina Knowles Says Beyonce’s Full Circle Moment at the Met Gala ‘Brought Tears to My Eyes’
summary: Beyoncé's mother, Tina Knowles, described how she felt watching her daughter return to the Met Gala, with granddaughter, Blue Ivy.
story: Beyoncé attended the Met Gala for the first time in a decade this year. It was an appearance that felt like déjà vu to her mother.

The “Halo” singer served as co-chair for the May 4 gala, which marked the first time she turned out for the event in 10 years. She was joined this year by husband Jay-Z and their daughter, Blue Ivy, 14.
“Oh, my God, I got really emotional. Shed a few tears,” Beyoncé’s mother, Tina Knowles, said when she appeared May 5 on the 3rd hour of TODAY.
Knowles said it was a full circle moment.

“It was kind of reminiscent, because Beyoncé started, you know, her first red carpet at 15, and Blue is 14. And so just seeing her out there looking confident and beautiful, it just brought tears to my eyes.”
Al Roker noted that Beyoncé and Blue “were like looking at both versions of the same (person) at the same time.”
“It’s like déjà vu,” Knowles added.
Knowles was also wowed by Sheinelle Jones dressing up as her daughter for TODAY’s Halloween spectacular last year.
“She was amazing,” Knowles said. “I posted it on my Instagram, and I couldn’t wait to show Beyoncé. She was so excited.”

Beyonce, who got a shoutout from Sheinelle at the Met Gala, has also spurred rumors that act three of her planned trilogy is coming any day now, even as TODAY.com has learned a release following 2023’s “Renaissance” and 2024’s “Cowboy Carter” is not imminent.
Don’t ask her mother, though, when to expect it.
“You know, I’ve said it before. I could tell you, but I’d have to kill you,” she joked.

title: Timothée Chalamet Was a No-Show at The Met Gala. Where Was He?
summary: The "Marty Supreme" star had to choose between the iconic fashion event and his beloved New York Knicks on May 4.
story: Timothée Chalamet had a choice to make on May 4: the fashion event of the year or his beloved New York Knicks.

The "Marty Supreme" star, who co-chaired the 2021 Met Gala and has been a regular at the iconic event, was faced with a dilemma when the Knicks eliminated the Atlanta Hawks in the first round of the NBA playoffs.
The first game of the Knicks' Eastern Conference semifinal series against the Philadelphia 76ers fell on the same day as the 2026 Met Gala, with both of them being held in his native New York City.

Chalamet, 30, ended up skipping the "Costume Art" theme at the Metropolitan Museum of Art for the blue-and-orange theme at a raucous, sold-out Madison Square Garden. He was rewarded for his decision when the Knicks obliterated the Sixers 137-98 to take a 1-0 lead in the series.
He posted a shot on his Instagram story of him resting his sneakers on the floor at MSG.

The actor probably didn't plan on facing the conflict with the Met Gala because the Sixers were an underdog to the Boston Celtics in the first round. Had the Celtics won, Monday night's game against the Knicks would have been in Boston. Instead, the Sixers stunned the Celtics in Game 7 of their first-round series, giving the Knicks homecourt advantage in the semifinals and making the first game at MSG.

This is the second straight year the Knicks have prompted Chalamet to skip the Met Gala. He was not there in 2025, either, and posted a shot on his Instagram story of him watching the Knicks' playoff game on an iPad at the time of the event.
Chalamet spoke with former Knicks star Carmelo Anthony on Anthony's 7PM in Brooklyn podcast in December 2025 about skipping the Met Gala that year.
"I had to be locked in, man," he said. "That Knicks' run last year was the most fun — I think that was the most fun period of my life, those four months. That was just the Garden being electric."
Chalamet's longtime girlfriend, Kylie Jenner, made sure the couple was represented at the 2026 Met Gala by stunning in a sleeveless Schiaparelli gown and what appeared to be bleached eyebrows.

Jenner, 28, has been a fixture at the Met Gala since her first appearance at the annual event in 2016. She was flying solo as Chalamet sat courtside at MSG to cheer on the Knicks' third straight playoff victory by 25 or more points.

Chalamet grew up as a diehard Knicks fan and has been a courtside fixture at MSG alongside regulars like Spike Lee, Tracy Morgan, Ben Stiller and Susie Essman. Lee has also been a former attendee at the Met Gala, but he was in attendance with the Knicks' faithful at MSG on Monday night.

title: Kellie Pickler Returns to ‘American Idol’ With 2006 Alums, 3 Years After Husband’s Death
summary: “American Idol” star Kellie Pickler returned to the show to perform with Hanna Harper on an episode celebrating the 30th anniversary of the show's fifth season.
story: “American Idol” welcomed back one of its own on the May 4 episode of the ABC stalwart.
Kellie Pickler, who finished in sixth place during the show’s fifth season in 2006, returned to the singing competition, as part of “Idol’s” celebration of that season.
Pickler performed with current semifinalist Hannah Harper, as the pair teamed up to sing Martina McBride’s “A Broken Wing.”
“I’m an antique ‘Idol’ dustin’ off,” the “Best Days of Your Life” singer said after the performance, according to E! Online.

“Being able to share the stage with you has been such a blessing,” she added, to Harper, who managed to avoid elimination, while advancing to the May 11 finale, along with Keyla Richardson and Jordan McCullough.

Pickler was joined on “Idol” by fellow finalists from the 2006 season, including Elliott Yamin, Paris Bennett, Bucky Covington and Taylor Hicks, who won that season. Each of them performed a duet with one of the five remaining hopefuls, while original “American Idol” judges Paula Abdul and Randy Jackson also came back to serve as guest judges.

Pickler’s “Idol” appearance marked her first time on the show since her husband, Kyle Jacobs, died in 2023 at the age of 49. Pickler has kept a low profile since then, last performing at a Patsy Cline tribute in 2024.
“One of the most beautiful lessons my husband taught me was in a moment of a crisis, 'if you don’t know what to do, do nothing, just be still,’” Pickler told People in August 2023.
“I have chosen to heed his advice.”

title: Blue Ivy Carter, 14, Becomes One of the Youngest Met Gala Attendees — See the Best Pics
summary: Blue Ivy Carter, daughter of Jay-Z and Beyoncé, attended the Met Gala 2026 in Balenciaga. She posed with her parents and alone.
story: Blue Ivy Carter was ready for her Met Gala moment.
The 14-year-old daughter of Beyoncé and Jay-Z made her debut at the iconic fashion event on May 4, dazzling in Balenciaga while appearing alongside her parents.

Her first Met Gala coincided with Beyoncé's return to the event for the first time in 10 years, as the "Texas Hold 'Em" singer was one of four co-chairs of the annual affair at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City.
Blue Ivy wore a billowing white gown with a long train that had a matching jacket, all of which was designed by Balenciaga.

She also had on sunglasses and silver stiletto heels as she posed alongside Jay-Z, who looked dapper in a black suit by Louis Vuitton, and Beyoncé, who stunned in a diamond-encrusted skeletal gown that was custom-made by Olivier Rousteing.

Blue Ivy's appearance was surprising in that she is the rare minor to attend the event. Organizers told The Hollywood Reporter in 2018 that the Met Gala "is not an appropriate event for people under 18," but exceptions are made for minors accompanied by their parents.
Blue Ivy was 4 years old the last time Beyoncé attended the Met Gala before Monday night, so her mother soaked in the special moment.
“It feels surreal because my daughter’s here,” Beyoncé told Vogue during a livestream of the event. “She looks so beautiful — it’s incredible to be able to share it with her.”
Blue Ivy looked like a seasoned veteran as she posed solo and with her parents.
“She was ready!" Beyoncé said on the livestream. "She is ready."

The teen is no stranger to sharing the spotlight with her mother, as she joined Beyoncé onstage during her "Cowboy Carter Tour" in 2024 and posed with her on the red carpet in 2023 at the London premiere of her "Renaissance" concert film.
She also made her big screen debut as the voice of Kiara in the 2024 animated movie "Mufasa: The Lion King."
Blue Ivy also has become known for her style, which she showed off in December while sitting courtside with Jay-Z at a Los Angeles Lakers game.

title: On the Met Gala Carpet, Rihanna Gave Sheinelle an Inspirational Talk She’ll Never Forget
summary: Rihanna shared words of encouragement for Sheinelle Jones on the red carpet of the Met Gala.
story: Sheinelle Jones got a seal of approval from one of the world’s biggest pop stars at the Met Gala on May 4.

While reporting from the red carpet in New York City for TODAY on fashion’s biggest night of the year, Sheinelle received some serious words of encouragement from Rihanna, who attended with her longtime partner, A$AP Rocky.
Revisit all the best moments from the 2026 Met Gala
“So, what was your day like today?” the singer asked.
“Today was, honestly — hoping that I could meet the moment and catch somebody’s eye contact,” Sheinelle answered.

Rihanna, who wore a sculpted gold and silver gown by Maison Margiela FW25 Couture with crystals and beads wrapped in a gold floor-length circular shawl, then replied with a strong dose of affirmation that touched on Sheinelle’s career, as well as her sense of style.

“Well, you deserve to be poured into, as well. And you’re beautiful. You’re a Black journalist. And I respect you. I love you for this. And, by the way, your outfit is f------ fire,” she told Sheinelle.
“I love you, Rihanna,” Sheinelle replied.

The theme for this year’s Met Gala was “Fashion Is Art,” which went hand in hand with the Metropolitan Museum of Art’s spring exhibition of “Costume Art.” This year’s gala once again featured a litany of A-list stars, from Anna Wintour and Dwayne Johnson to Amanda Seyfried and Beyoncé, who was making her first appearance at the event in a decade, while co-chairing this year’s festivities.

Sheinelle was determined to see the Grammy-winning star, who wore a custom Olivier Rousteing gown covered in a silver crystals and was accompanied by husband Jay-Z and their 14-year-old daughter, Blue Ivy.
“Beyoncé, can I just tell you that we celebrate you?” Sheinelle shouted to her as the “Texas Hold ‘Em” singer posed for photos. “We celebrate you.”
It appeared as if Beyoncé mouthed something to Sheinelle, which delighted her.
“All I wanted was eye contact and a loving gaze — I’m happy,” Sheinelle said.

title: Mariska Hargitay Reveals New Bob Haircut Ahead of Broadway Debut — See the Pics
summary: Mariska Hargitay just ditched her long locks for something a bit breezier.
story: Mariska Hargitay just ditched her long locks for something a bit breezier.
The "Law & Order: Special Victims Unit" star debuted a brand new bob haircut in a series of photos posted by her hairstylist, Adir Abergel.
The fresh cut comes just in time for the actor’s Broadway debut in the solo show “Every Brilliant Thing” this month. Hargitay will be taking over for Daniel Radcliffe on May 26.

Abergel hinted that Hargitay's upcoming stint on Broadway inspired the look in his post's caption, writing, "NEW CUT FOR THE ONE AND ONLY @therealmariskahargitay BROADWAY DEBUT IN @brilliantbway ❤️❤️❤️❤️."
Hargitay also commented on the post, writing, "Switching gears."
Abergel highlighted his handiwork in the photo series, which showed Hargitay's chin-length, wavy bob in all its glory.
The actor's fans reacted to her new look in the comments section.
"It should be criminal to look this good!" the official Instagram page for “Law & Order” wrote.
One fan commented, "OMG!!! This is incredible on her 👏👏👏."
Another wrote: "OBSESSED with the bob!!! It is so so good. How rude of Mariska to be able to pull off basically anything and look absolutely fabulous."

Abergel just shared photos of Hargitay's new hairstyle, but the actor gave fans a first look at the haircut on April 26 in a video promoting her Broadway debut. In the short clip, she danced at a photoshoot with confetti covering the floor.

In March, Playbill shared details about Hargitay's upcoming role in "Every Brilliant Thing," a solo play that first debuted at Edinburgh Festival Fringe. In the play, the protagonist "faces a distant father, a suicidal mother, and his own inexplicable deep sadness."

"I read 'Every Brilliant Thing' and cried, rejoiced, laughed, cried some more, and loved it so much,” Hargitay said in a statement to Playbill. "I’m always drawn to themes of healing and renewal, especially when the journey is rendered in all its complexity. It feels like an extraordinary gift to make my Broadway debut, the fulfillment of a lifelong dream, with a play that affirms life so emphatically."
Hargitay's first performance will take place on May 26. The show will run through June 28.

title: Dolly Parton Reveals She Is Canceling Vegas Residency Due to Health Issues: 'A Little Bad News'
summary: Dolly Parton is sharing an encouraging update on her ongoing health struggles, but it came with "a little bad news" too, she said: She is canceling her upcoming Las Vegas residency."The good news is: I'm responding really well to meds and treatments and I'm improving every day," she said in a video posted to her Instagram on May 4."Now the bad news is: It’s going to take me a little while before I'm up to stage performance level because some of the meds and treatments make me a little bit 'swi
story: Dolly Parton is sharing an encouraging update on her ongoing health struggles, but it came with "a little bad news" too, she said: She is canceling her upcoming Las Vegas residency.
"The good news is: I'm responding really well to meds and treatments and I'm improving every day," she said in a video posted to her Instagram on May 4.

"Now the bad news is: It’s going to take me a little while before I'm up to stage performance level because some of the meds and treatments make me a little bit 'swimmy' headed, as my grandma used to say," Parton said in the video.
The 80-year-old went on to compare herself to “an old classic car that once restored can be better than ever.”

“But when they raised the hood on this old antique, they realized that I need to rebuild my engine and that my transmission is slipping, my oil pan is leaking and my muffler’s busted and my shocks and pistons need to be replaced,” she said.
More specifically, Parton said she has "always had problems" with kidney stones.

"Lord, they dig more stones out of me a year than the rock quarry in Rockwood, Tennessee," she joked. "But seriously, my immune system and my digestive system got all out of whack over the past couple three years, and they're working real hard on rebuilding and strengthening those. And hopefully, I'll be up to snuff again soon."
She did not share further details on her health struggles.
Parton said she's trying to keep everything "light and airy" and has been keeping busy working on several professional projects as she navigates her health challenges.
The singer previously had to postpone her Vegas concerts in September 2025 as she focused on her health.
"On a more serious note, I'm truly sorry that I'm going to miss all of you that had tickets to see me in Las Vegas," she said.
During her video message, Parton also thanked her fans for their support after her husband, Carl Dean, died in 2025.
"I will always love him, and I'll always miss him, but you would be surprised at how much your love and concern meant to me during that time," she said.
At the end of her video message, Parton said she has "great doctors" and is "doing really well."
"And they assure me that everything I have is treatable. So I'm going with that," she said.

title: The 2026 Met Gala Guest List: Here Are All the Stars Who Attended
summary: It's the first Monday in May and that means fashion’s biggest night out is officially here
story: It's the first Monday in May and that means fashion’s biggest night out is officially here.
The 2026 Met Gala is expected, per usual, to be a star-studded and A-list affair, with some of the biggest and most impressive names in music, movies, sports, fashion and more in attendance.
Follow live coverage

The guest list was shared on Monday afternoon with the media lining up at the red carpet. This came after a slew of stars were already revealed to be attending as the gala’s co-chairs and committee members for 2026.
This year’s co-chairs Beyoncé, Venus Williams and Nicole Kidman, as well as Anna Wintour, now in her new role as Condé Nast’s chief content officer.
Kidman and Williams attended the 2025 Met Gala, but this will be Beyoncé's first appearance in a decade.

The four chairs will be joined by Anthony Vaccarello and Zoë Kravitz, who will also co-chair the celebration's host committee that includes additional celebrities who will undoubtedly be dressed to the nines in designer wear.

The 2026 Met Gala theme is “Costume Art,” with the dress code described as “fashion is art.” Beyoncé, Kidman, Williams and the rest of the VIP guests are expected to interpret the theme and dress code by expressing “their own relationship to fashion as an embodied artform,” according to Vogue’s guidelines.

At last year's Met Gala, Rihanna surprised guests, fans and Met Gala viewers by revealing her pregnancy, André 3000 literally carried a piano on his back, and Bad Bunny honored his Puerto Rican roots, while Zendaya and Anna Sawai had nearly identical looks.
Expect many jaw-dropping, head-turning, inspirational and, perhaps, shocking looks as the stars make their way up the celebrated stairs at the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Below, see a list of 2026 Met Gala attendees. This story will be updated with photos as guests arrive.
Met Gala 2026 Guest List

title: Rihanna (Again) Steals the Show at 2026 Met Gala
summary: Rihanna arrived fashionably late to the Met Gala on the arm of longtime love A$AP Rocky
story: Rihanna arrived fashionably late to the Met Gala on the arm of longtime love A$AP Rocky.

Rihanna donned a sculptural gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela FW25 Couture, featuring a long-sleeve top embellished in crystals and beads. She was wrapped in a ribbony gold shawl that reached down to the floor and resembled a golden cocoon.
Follow for live updates
She completed her look with metallic curls woven into her hair and large rings and other pieces of eye-catching jewelry from Briony Raymond and Dyne jewelry.
A$AP Rocky wore a pink Chanel tuxedo with black lapels.
The couple have made the annual event a kind of family affair over the years.
Rihanna stole the show on the red carpet of the 2025 Met Gala by revealing she was expecting her third child, Rocki Irish, who was born in September 2025.
Actually, it was A$AP Rocky who broke the pregnancy news just ahead of the "Diamonds" singer's entrance.
“Rihanna’s outfit tonight is ... I don’t know, whatever, just don’t cover her baby hump, you dig?” he said in an interview on the carpet.
Rihanna strolled into the gala with a look that was picture perfect for the night's theme, “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style.”
She wore a cropped jacket with a crossover necktie and a gray corseted top that did cover her baby bump.
Her floor-length skirt resembled a suit jacket, and she topped it all off with a wide-brim black hat.
As she made her way to speak with TODAY’s Al Roker, a fan jokingly called out to ask Rihanna if she’d brought her “umbrella-ella-ella, eh” — a play on her hit 2007 single “Umbrella.”
“I brought the kid!” Rihanna responded.
Rihanna has earned raves for her Met Gala looks over the years.

The singer and Fenty mogul has been a steady presence at the event since making her gala debut in 2007. She wore a white Georges Chakra dress that she paired with black fishnet gloves for her first outing.
In 2009, she wore a Dolce & Gabba tux with puffed sleeves. She topped off the look with slicked back hair.

In 2011, she put her own spin on that year's “Alexander McQueen: Savage Beauty” theme by wearing a curve-hugging lace Stella McCartney gown paired with long red hair done up in a braid over her shoulder.
For 2015's “China: Through the Looking Glass” theme, she wore a regal Guo Pei-designed yellow coat-dress with long train and crown.
In 2017, she wore a wild sculptural floral Commes des Garçons gown for the gala's “Rei Kawakubo: Art Of The In-Between” theme.

In 2018, she took fashion cues from the Vatican for the gala's “Heavenly Bodies: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination” theme. She wore a rhinestone-encrusted minidress with a coat — and, yes, a papal headdress — designed by John Galliano for Maison Margiela.
In 2021, Rihanna wore a Balenciaga overcoat that she paired with  267 carats of Bulgari jewelry.
In 2023, she arrived an hour and a half late to pay homage to Karl Lagerfeld by wearing a custom white hooded Valentino Haute Couture gown adorned with camellia flowers and a long train.
The singer later removed the outer part of her ensemble to reveal a strappy white gown and a baby bump, after revealing her pregnancy during her Super Bowl halftime show.

title: Heidi Klum Went Just as Hard at the Met Gala as She Does for Halloween
summary: Heidi Klum brought her love of Halloween costumes to the 2026 Met Gala
story: Heidi Klum brought her love of Halloween costumes to the 2026 Met Gala.
Klum, who throws one of Hollywood’s most famous Halloween parties every year, got into the spooky spirit to honor the event’s “Fashion is Art” dress code this year.
Follow for live updates

The 52-year-old supermodel and “Project Runway” host arrived at the gala dressed as a marble statue in a custom look created by her longtime Halloween collaborator, Oscar-nominated make-up artist Mike Marino, Klum revealed on Instagram.
The look was inspired by classical sculptures such as “Veiled Vestal” by Raffaele Monti.
Marino “transformed fabric into sculpture, manipulating latex and spandex with extraordinary precision to mirror the stillness, delicacy, and illusion of carved marble,” Klum explained.
Klum appeared to be carved out of marble and then draped with a gauzy white sheet. She wore ancient-looking sandals on her feet, a simple wreath on her head and creepy looking contact lenses.
Klum also expressed her enthusiasm for this year’s gala dress code.
“I love fashion, I love art, and I especially love when the two collide,” she wrote.

Klum’s Met Gala look isn’t too far a cry from her 2025 Halloween costume. The model transformed into Medusa, the terrifying figure from Greek mythology who has snakes instead of hair. So frightening looking was Medusa that anyone who dared look at her turned to stone.
For the costume, Klum wore a prosthetic forked tongue and had fake snakes moving around on her head. Scales covered her face and body and she wore bright green contact lenses.
Klum’s husband Tom Kaulitz walked the red carpet with her dressed as one of Medusa’s victims: a stone figure.
Klum told People magazine in 2025 that she sees creating her costumes each year as a kind of “art.”

“I never take the easy way out,” she said. “People say, ‘Why the heck are you doing this?’ I do it for the art of it, of transformation, of surprising people also. It’s like a life performance art thing in a way because you see so much AI and things are being photographed, repatched, this, that and the other.”
“I always try to come up with something that is giving people an ‘ooh’ and ‘ahh’ and a wow because I want to inspire people to also do something creative,” she shares.

title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire near the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire near the Strait of Hormuz, raising questions about the negotiations to end the conflict.
story: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire near the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, raising questions about the negotiations to end the conflict.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
President Donald Trump said on Truth Social that the attacks happened as three U.S. military ships were transiting through the strait.

“Three World Class American Destroyers just transited, very successfully, out of the Strait of Hormuz, under fire. There was no damage done to the three Destroyers, but great damage done to the Iranian attackers. They were completely destroyed along with numerous small boats, which are being used to take the place of their fully decapitated Navy. These boats went to the bottom of the Sea, quickly and efficiently. Missiles were shot at our Destroyers, and were easily knocked down. Likewise, drones came, and were incinerated while in the air. They dropped ever so beautifully down to the Ocean, very much like a butterfly dropping to its grave!” he wrote.

Trump added: “A normal Country would have allowed these Destroyers to pass, but Iran is not a normal Country. They are led by LUNATICS, and if they had the chance to use a Nuclear Weapon, they would do it, without question — But they’ll never have that opportunity and, just like we knocked them out again today, we’ll knock them out a lot harder, and a lot more violently, in the future, if they don’t get their Deal signed, FAST! Our three Destroyers, with their wonderful Crews, will now rejoin our Naval Blockade, which is truly a ‘Wall of Steel.’”
The attacks highlighted the fragility of the ceasefire in the area around the Strait of Hormuz, which 20% of the world’s oil used to pass through before the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28.

No ships transited the strait Thursday, the second day in a row that the critical waterway has had no traffic at all, according to S&P Global Market Intelligence. It’s also the first time ​since March 12-13 that there have been two back-to-back days without marine traffic.
Trump said in an interview with ABC News on Thursday: “The ceasefire is going. It’s in effect.”
He added, “It’s just a love tap.”
Trump later told a group of reporters that negotiations with Iran were “going very well, but they have to understand if it doesn’t get signed, they’re going to have a lot of pain.”
Trump said a proposal had been given to Iran that included an agreement that it would not have nuclear weapons and that it would give “the nuclear dust” to the U.S.
“Yeah, they’ve agreed. But when they agree, it doesn’t mean much, because the next day they forget,” he said.
Trump also said a deal “might not happen, but it could happen any day.”
A U.S. official told NBC News that the U.S. strikes in Bandar Abbas and Qeshm Island on Thursday were defensive and do not constitute a resumption of major combat operations against Iran.
U.S. Central Command said on X that three U.S. Navy guided-missile destroyers came under attack as they transited the Strait of Hormuz to the Gulf of Oman and responded with “self-defense strikes.”
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
CENTCOM said the Iranian military “launched multiple missiles, drones and small boats” at the vessels.

“No U.S. assets were struck. U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) eliminated inbound threats and targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces including missile and drone launch sites; command and control locations; and intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance nodes. CENTCOM does not seek escalation but remains positioned and ready to protect American forces.”
The Iranian military said it fired at U.S. military ships only after the U.S. military violated the ceasefire and attacked an Iranian oil tanker.

“The invading, terrorist and pirate U.S. military violated the ceasefire by targeting an Iranian oil tanker moving from Iran’s coastal waters in the Jask area towards the Strait of Hormuz, as well as another vessel entering the Strait of Hormuz opposite the port of Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates,” said the spokesperson for Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters, according to the semiofficial Mehr news agency.
The spokesman also said the U.S. had carried out air attacks on “civilian areas” along Iran’s coast and Qeshm Island.
Iran’s state-owned Press TV later reported that following the exchange of fire, the situation on Iran’s islands and in coastal cities near the Strait of Hormuz was “back to normal now.”

title: DOJ probing trades in oil futures and prediction markets just ahead of major Iran war news
summary: The Justice Department and the CFTC are probing a series of highly profitable oil market trades made just ahead of major policy announcements on the Iran war.
story: The Justice Department and the country’s top commodity market regulator are investigating a series of oil market trades just ahead of major policy announcements from President Donald Trump and Iranian officials, according to a source familiar with the probe.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The statements and social media posts all pertained to the ongoing U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
ABC News first reported the investigation earlier Thursday; Reuters first reported the trades themselves in mid-April.

In addition to examining the oil markets for potential insider trading, the source told NBC News that investigators are also pursuing a separate probe into suspicious activity on prediction market platforms, also related to Iran war developments.
The source said that the investigations are in their early stages and that there is no conclusive evidence yet of criminal wrongdoing.
Some of the oil trades happened before Trump announced he would delay attacks on Iran’s power grids, before talks of a ceasefire and ahead of key developments in the Strait of Hormuz.

The Justice Department and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission are looking into at least four trades in which the traders made a total of more than $2.6 billion betting that oil prices would drop right before they did, a person familiar with the matter told NBC News.
The trades are said to have been placed in March and April.
The Justice Department investigation is being led by the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Southern District of New York.
The Justice Department declined to comment on the investigation. The CFTC did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
CME Group, which owns NYMEX, the exchange on which U.S. crude oil futures trade, declined to comment.
Intercontinental Exchange, which owns the London-based exchange on which international Brent futures trade, declined to comment.
The U.K.’s Financial Conduct Authority, contacted outside normal business hours in London, did not immediately comment.

title: Iran-backed groups targeted U.S. facilities in Iraq 600 times, U.S. official says
summary: The U.S. is urging the government to break with Tehran-backed militia groups following more than 600 attacks against U.S. diplomatic facilities.
story: WASHINGTON - The U.S. is urging the Iraqi government to break with Tehran-backed militia groups and has sanctioned Iraqi officials over their ties with Iran, following more than 600 attacks against U.S. diplomatic facilities in Iraq since the start of the U.S. and Israel-led war, according to the State Department and a senior state department official.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

One facility, the Baghdad Diplomatic Support Center, which is a logistics hub for U.S. diplomats, has been targeted by repeated drone strikes and was hit in mid-March. As recently as Tuesday, the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad urged Americans to leave Iraq, warning that “militias continue to plot additional attacks against U.S. citizens and targets associated with the United States throughout Iraq.”
Videos from some of the attacks in Baghdad show smoke rising from a facility near the airport in April, and a fire on the roof of the embassy in March.

The State Department on Thursday announced it sanctioned Iraq’s Deputy Minister of Oil Ali Maarij al-Bahadly, arguing he had abused his position to divert Iraqi oil in support of the Iranian regime. Iranian oil was mixed with Iraqi oil and sold for Iran’s benefit, spokesman Tommy Pigott said. Oil sector companies that support militias were also hit with sanctions.
Iraq’s embassy in Washington did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The move is the latest effort by the Trump administration to manage the growing presence of Iran inside Iraq, where Tehran-linked militia groups that would like to see U.S. troops expelled from the country have gained a foothold in the government.

title: Boy, 14, shot dead by Israeli settlers in West Bank amid escalation in violence
summary: AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead
story: AL-MUGHAYYIR, OCCUPIED WEST BANK – A boy’s blood still stained the sidewalk in front of his school, days after an Israeli settler shot him dead.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
Aws al-Nasaan, 14, was gunned down in broad daylight last week in the small Palestinian village of Al-Mughayyir, in the occupied West Bank.

His death comes amid a dramatic escalation in settler violence, alongside a push to expand Israel's control over the West Bank through new laws, settlement expansions and security crackdowns limiting Palestinians' freedom. According to data from the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, Israeli settlers and forces have killed at least 42 Palestinians so far this year.

The attack, caught on video from multiple angles, took place just after noon on Tuesday, April 21. Video obtained by NBC News shows a bearded man in an Israeli military uniform firing down the hill toward the school. He crouches to get a better angle and keeps shooting.
Video shot from the road to the east side of the school shows bullets hitting the wall. In one of the videos, al-Nasaan’s body can be seen on the ground as his friends shout for help.

“They ran out of the gates trying to escape,” the school’s principal Bassem Abu Assaf said, pointing out the bullet holes still visible in the stone wall last week. “The shooting was crazy, just crazy, nonstop shooting.”
Construction worker Jihad Abu Naim, 32, was also killed in the attack, the principal said.

“This is supposed to be a safe place,” said Abu Assaf, who is responsible for 460 students. “School is a right for every child,” he added. But, fearful of another attack, classes were cancelled for a week.

Palestinian eyewitnesses say Israeli soldiers were on the scene within minutes and did nothing to stop or apprehend the shooter. Instead, they lobbed tear gas into the crowd of students and villagers outside of the school, Abu Assaf and multiple eyewitnesses said.
Paramedics killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon

In response to questions from NBC News about the Al-Mughayyir attack, the Israeli military confirmed the shooter was a reservist soldier and said an investigation has been launched. A statement said: “The IDF reservist has been suspended from reserve duty pending the conclusion of the investigation and his weapon has been confiscated.”
According to rights groups, Israeli settler violence has surged in the occupied West Bank in recent weeks.

Israeli human rights group Yesh Din documented nearly 400 incidents in the first 40 days of the Iran war. Incidents, many caught on video, include shootings, beatings, arson, land destruction, livestock theft and intimidation.
“The root reason is that everyone is looking elsewhere,” the organization’s CEO, Ziv Stahl said of the record spike in violence. “The world is distracted.”

title: U.S. monitoring hantavirus cruise passengers; dozens left ship after first death
summary: Health officials in at least a dozen countries, including the U.S., are tracking dozens of passengers who traveled aboard the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak
story: Health officials in at least a dozen countries, including the U.S., are tracking dozens of passengers who traveled aboard the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Those passengers have dispersed across the world and were in five states as of Thursday afternoon: Arizona, California, Georgia, Texas and Virginia. Health officials in those states said the former passengers, who are not being publicly identified, have not shown any symptoms.
Despite the widening international response, World Health Organization officials say the outbreak is not the start of a new pandemic or epidemic.
“While this is a serious incident, WHO assesses the public health risk as low,” Director General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said in a statement Thursday.

The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has said it and the State Department are closely monitoring the status of Americans on the ship, adding that “risk to the American public is extremely low.”
The atmosphere on board
A passenger still on board the ship described the general mood as calm now that travelers who were exposed have connected with doctors.
“Everyone is keeping high spirit, people are smiling and taking the situation calmly,” passenger Kasem Ibn Hattuta said in an email Thursday.
Medical teams have boarded the cruise, Hattuta added, which is contributing to a sense of normalcy after days of worry and concern.

“I’ve seen several people walking on the outer decks breathing fresh air and some birders trying to spot the different seabirds on the way, but everyone is wearing masks specially when inside the ship and keeping a safe distance,” Hattuta said.
Those who remain on the MV Hondius are being asked to stay in their cabins, the WHO said. Cabins are being disinfected, and anyone who shows symptoms will be isolated.
So far, the people still on the vessel have not shown any symptoms.
Dr. Stephen Kornfeld, a passenger, said that when the ship’s doctor became ill, he had to step in.
“As things deteriorated and more people got sick and more got seriously ill, I ended up just taking over and trying to keep everybody going,” he told news station KTVZ of Oregon.
Kornfeld said the situation has improved since patients were evacuated.
“Fortunately, nobody else has gotten sick in the last six to seven days,” the doctor said, adding that other experts have come on board.
“We now have two World Health Organization epidemiologists on the boat. We have two Dutch infectious disease people on the boat. So there’s a lot of medical coverage now. “
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

Dr. Martin Kriz, who has worked on the MV Hondius and its sister ship, said self-isolating aboard a small cruise of that size is nearly impossible because of shared spaces including lounges and dining rooms.
“You can’t do much if you’re far from land,” Kriz said. “You do what you can do.”
Cruise ships typically follow a three-tiered protocol for handling infectious outbreaks, he added. The first involves passengers wearing masks, and the highest level calls for passengers to isolate.
Passengers who disembarked

Weeks after the first death on April 11, 29 passengers disembarked on the remote Atlantic island St. Helena without undergoing contact tracing, cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions said in a statement. The dead person was also removed from the vessel.
The company said it was “working to establish” the whereabouts of all those who disembarked April 24 in St. Helena and had contacted all of them. It said the number included six Americans.
Three people aboard the luxury cruise have died. There are five confirmed cases and three suspected infections, according to the WHO.

One person who was on board the MV Hondius is at home in Arizona, another is in Virginia, two are in Georgia, two are in Texas, and an unknown number are back in California, according to authorities in those states. None have reported symptoms of the rare virus, which has an incubation period of up to six weeks. Officials say the risk to the public remains low.
The outbreak is believed to have started following a birdwatching expedition from Argentina to Cape Verde.

According to the company, 114 guests were aboard the ship on April 1 following departure from Ushuaia, Argentina. On April 15, six additional guests joined at Tristan da Cunha, between Ushuaia and St. Helena, bringing the total to 120. At this time, the deceased passenger is included in the tally.
The passengers who left the boat comprise 12 nationalities; the home countries of two people are unknown.
Flight attendant tested
Dutch officials said Thursday that a hospitalized flight attendant who was not a passenger was being tested for hantavirus in the Netherlands.

“I can confirm that a stewardess is in hospital now and she is being tested for the virus,” a spokesperson for the Dutch Health Ministry said. The department did not say whether she was ill or showing symptoms of the virus, which is rare but potentially deadly.

KLM Royal Dutch Airlines said Wednesday that a Dutch woman who died after she contracted hantavirus was “briefly” on a flight from Johannesburg, South Africa, to Amsterdam and was removed from the plane before takeoff.
It is not clear whether the flight attendant was on the same flight. KLM said in a statement it does not comment on individual cases “for privacy reasons.”
A look at the virus
WHO and national health officials have said person-to-person transmission is possible through close personal contact, such as between couples.

Hantavirus is typically contracted through contact with rodents. The WHO confirmed this week that the outbreak is the Andes strain of the virus, which, unlike other strains, can spread between people through close personal contact.
Health experts have said even that strain is not as easily transmitted as airborne diseases such as influenza or Covid-19.
Hantavirus infection in humans is extremely rare and has never been previously recorded on a cruise ship. CDC data shows there were 890 confirmed cases in the U.S. from 1993 to 2023.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

Argentine authorities said that a rodent-trapping program would take place in the city of Ushuaia, where the MV Hondius began its journey, and that they would conduct 2,500 diagnostic tests to identify the outbreak’s origin.

A travel vlogger aboard the vessel recorded a video of the captain telling passengers that the ship was “not infectious” after having announced the first death. He told NBC News that passengers were “not well informed” about the unfolding situation.

Oceanwide Expeditions addressed a video that was circulating of the captain’s announcement, saying it was made before the person’s cause of death was known and that at the time “no evidence of a virus or contagion was present on the vessel.”
Where is the ship headed?

The ship is heading north from Cape Verde, off the western coast of Africa, to the Spanish-controlled Canary Islands. The journey is expected to last three to four days, though the leader of the islands’ regional government is reluctant to accept the ship.
Spain’s Interior Ministry said that the ship was expected to arrive Sunday and that evacuations would begin Monday, “if all goes well.”

Virginia Barcones, the secretary general of civil protection and emergencies at the Interior Ministry, said at a news conference Thursday that the U.S. will send an aircraft to pick up the 17 American passengers still aboard the ship.
The three people who have died are a Dutch couple and a German national. A British man is being treated in a hospital in South Africa.
It emerged Wednesday that a man who had left the ship was being treated in Zurich with suspected hantavirus.

Three patients were transported from the MV Hondius on Wednesday for medical treatment in the Netherlands and Germany as health officials shift their focus to locating and monitoring the dozens of people who left the ship along its voyage.

title: U.S. departure from WHO could hinder hantavirus response
summary: The U.S. left the World Health Organization in January. Because of that, experts say, it might not get immediate access to information about the hantavirus outbreak on a Dutch-operated cruise ship.
story: The deadly hantavirus outbreak aboard a Dutch-operated cruise ship with passengers of 23 nationalities is precisely the type of public health crisis the World Health Organization is designed to tackle. But the U.S. formally left the WHO in January, after 78 years of membership.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

As a result, health experts say, the U.S. might not get immediate access to surveillance data about the virus and contact tracing information about cases linked to the ship, which could help prevent additional infections.
“The access that we got as being members and as being important donors to WHO is gone,” said Amira Albert Roess, a professor of global health and epidemiology at George Mason University.
Ordinarily, Roess said, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention experts would have been part of the teams sequencing the virus. Now, the U.S. might have to learn about results secondhand.

When asked about those concerns, the Department of Health and Human Services referred NBC News to a statement on the CDC website, which says that the State Department has been in direct contact with cruise ship passengers and that the government is “working closely with our international partners to provide technical assistance and guidance to mitigate risk.”

“The White House continues to work with the CDC and State Department to monitor and respond to a recent outbreak of the Andes virus,” White House spokesman Kush Desai said in a statement. “While possible risks to the American public remain low, the CDC has convened leading experts on Andes virus to assist in this effort to ensure Americans are protected. The entire Administration remains vigilant.”
